reviews.review_msg: (BUS 207) Solid choice. Only midterms and one final for this class. Explains concepts well in detail and goes over practice problems in lecture. Pre recorded videos posted on canvas and optional lecture that is recorded where he goes over notes, questions, and problems in detail.

Great professor so far. Really knowledgeable on the subject and manages to explain the ideas in an easy-to-understand manner. The course isn't too difficult if you follow along with the lectures since the prof provides several examples and practice questions which are often harder than the examples seen on the midterms.
He's definitely a professor who teaches. But super handsome guy, definitely a dilf makes class interesting .
Worst professor ive ever taken. Lacks empathy and doesn't know what hes talking about.

Great person with the right intentions; however, Colin's Bus 345 class is harder than I expected it would be.  The amount you are expected to learn is overwhelming and if taking this class with Colin, it is highly recommended that your overall course load is light.
Hard class due to high level of student knowledge

Good professor, but overloads his students with work. Too hard for most people, difficult class, not recommended unless you have a background in it or would like to become am master of this, but great guy, and a good professor,I recommend taking this class and no other that semester.

Business is a very practical discipline so Colin lacks the industry experience that profs who had them would be able to share. I found this course material (not the prof!) to be pretty useless. It's a required course, but I honestly can't say I've learned very much. But on the other hand he does make lectures interesting and uses lots of multimedia
He is smart, nice, and hot! Totally doesn't fit into the triangle theory in the text book (p.277)!
